text: fat is 3.4g. omega 3 is doing serious work here. for a grain, carb quality matters a lot.
carbs is 71.5g. the fibre support does a lot of the heavy lifting. for grains, the real question is whether those carbs come with enough fibre and stability.
protein is 13.3g. protein quality is clearly one of the selling points. protein helps, but grains still need the carb side to stay under control.
vitamin e at 1% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.
magnesium at 55% DV, iron at 12% DV, zinc at 20% DV stand out most. that is useful, but still not a huge mineral edge.

the upsides first. useful staple energy source because that matters because grains are often judged by how practical they are in real diets. works as a meal anchor because a lot of value comes from being easy to build around. usually affordable and scalable because practicality matters for staple foods.

now the downsides. protein quality is limited because even the better grains usually need pairing. carb load is still the main burden because that is the category risk no grain fully escapes. quality can drop fast with processing because the grain category punishes refinement pretty hard.
Buckwheat is A tier. Best when you want a staple carb that actually brings something useful with it.
